element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game - immediately upon leaving starting area
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Speed stat affects turn order probability; turn-based rhythm established early

element_introduction_order.element: Spirit System (Fire, Earth, Water, Air, Sphere, Sky, Heart, Light)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game - as party members gain access to spirits
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Eight spirits unlock individually, then 28 pair combinations, then 1 final spell with all eight - progressive unlocking structure rewards exploration and repeated casting

element_introduction_order.first_appears: First battle or early combat encounter
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental game tension: healing costs life, creates strategic pressure and risk-reward decisions; only one direct heal spell exists and kills the caster

element_introduction_order.element: Healing Bottles (9-use consumables, refillable at towns)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource management core to pacing; cheap refills at towns provide relief stations

element_introduction_order.element: Mercenary Recruitment (Up to 2 additional party members)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: New, stronger mercenaries become available as story progresses; player choice to level old or hire new creates progression flexibility

element_introduction_order.element: Equipment-Based Healing (Unlimited but self-only, out-of-battle unusable)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Mid-game as equipment upgrades become available
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Secondary healing path with significant restrictions; reinforces HP-drain tension

element_introduction_order.element: Story Encounters (Dal Gren, Zaygos threat, character recruitment)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Framing narrative from opening; progresses through mid to late game
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Mercenaries tied to story events have unique spirits and abilities unavailable to main characters - creates urgency around recruitment decisions

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching a town (healing bottle refill stations)
Acquiring new mercenary with better stats/spirits
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical resource-scarcity tension: encounters drain healing bottles → players must backtrack to towns for refills → refill provides relief → advance further, encounter stronger enemies → repeat. Tension ramps through mid-game as enemy difficulty exceeds party capability without optimal spirit combinations and mercenary hiring.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Mercenary availability increases in certain towns at story progression points; some mercenaries only available after specific story events, creating location-based gating

difficulty_curve.early_game: Moderate; players learn HP-drain mechanic and basic spirit casting; healing bottles readily available and refillable
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Rising tension; enemy stats increase faster than typical leveling, mercenary hiring becomes strategic necessity; reviewers noted characters felt underpowered, requiring tedious backtracking
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained high tension; late-game enemies and boss Dal Gren encounter demand optimized spirit combinations and full party coordination
notable_spikes: First major boss or Dal Gren encounter
Story event mercenary unavailability (single mercenary cannot be rehired after one story event)
Zaygos conflict encounters (main antagonist story escalation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Overworld navigation between towns (relief/resource) and dungeons (tension/encounters). Towns provide healing bottle refills and mercenary recruitment opportunities; dungeons are random-encounter gauntlets that drain resources.

Eight base spirits unlock as party gains experience. Each new spirit combination creates new spell options (1 individual = 1 spell; 2 spirits = +1 spell; 8 spirits = +1 spell = 37 total). Incentivizes revisiting early battles with new spirit pairs to increase mastery.

Stronger mercenaries become available at story milestones. Player can either continue leveling weaker mercenaries or hire new ones. Story-locked mercenary creates single forced decision point with permanent consequence (cannot rehire after specific event).
